Task description explicitly states this output path
Task description specifies 'Vanilla ice cream but did NOT get ill'
Task description specifies 'did NOT get ill'

Oswego outbreak 1940 dataset: Cohort analysis shows 43 people ate vanilla ice cream, with 11 becoming ill and 32 not becoming ill. Contingency table: Vanilla=Yes & Ill=No = 32 people. https://rdrr.io/cran/epitools/man/oswego.html and https://www.rdocumentation.org/packages/epitools/versions/0.5-10.1/topics/oswego

privileged_info_summary: The Oswego outbreak dataset (from April 18, 1940, church supper in Lycoming, NY) contains 75 interviewed individuals, of which 46 became ill with gastrointestinal illness caused by contaminated vanilla ice cream containing Staphylococcus aureus. The filter criteria (Vanilla=Yes AND Ill=No) correctly identifies people who ate vanilla ice cream but did NOT get ill. However, the row count is contradicted: the verified number is 32 people, not 18. According to cohort analysis of the Oswego dataset, 43 people ate vanilla ice cream: 11 became ill (attack rate 80%) and 32 did not become ill.
